title: I'm overwhelmed — where do I even begin?

messages.content: This is my first cafe. I have 15 active signals and 182 tasks. I feel completely lost.

I hear you. 182 tasks looks terrifying on screen, but you're not going to do 182 things. You're going to do 5 things this week, and then 5 more next week. That's how this works.

**This week, do these 5 things and nothing else:**

1. **Print and post the allergen matrix** (B005) — download the template, fill it in, laminate it, put it where staff can see it. 1 hour.

2. **Check your fridge temperatures right now** (B013) — if you don't have a thermometer, buy one today. Log temps morning and evening. Tape the log to the fridge door.

3. **Write down who does what during peak** (B166) — on one sheet of paper: Person A does X, Person B does Y. Post it next to the schedule.

4. **Set a table-clear target** — tell your team: tables cleared within 3 minutes. That's it. One rule.

5. **Do a 5-minute briefing before every shift** — what's the special, any 86'd items, who's on what station. Stand up, say it, start work.

That's 5 things. Maybe 4 hours of total work. You'll resolve or improve at least 4 of your 15 signals.

Everything else — the L2 tasks, the L3 strategic stuff — we'll get to it. But not until these 5 are done. Deal?
Deal. Starting with the fridge temps — I actually don't think our thermometer works.

Then your first task just got even simpler: **buy a thermometer**. A basic digital fridge thermometer costs about 10-15 EUR. Get one that shows current temp and min/max since last reset.

While you're at it, grab a second one for the other unit if you have two fridges. And a pack of logbook sheets — or just print a simple grid:

| Date | AM Temp (Fridge 1) | AM Temp (Fridge 2) | PM Temp (F1) | PM Temp (F2) | Initials |

Legal requirement in the EU: fridge must maintain below 5°C. If you see anything above that, move perishables and investigate immediately.

This is the kind of thing that seems small but prevents a food safety officer from shutting you down. Get the thermometer today. Log starts tomorrow morning.
